seoTitle: Add a Background Video in Webflow
metaDescription: How to add a looping background video to a hero section in Webflow, with mobile fallback images and text overlays.
intro: A looping background video can add real polish to a hero section, but it needs to be handled carefully to avoid hurting load speed or breaking on mobile. Webflow's native Background Video element handles the encoding and layering, letting you place text and buttons directly over the footage. This guide covers the full setup.

steps.heading: Add the Background Video element
steps.body: Click the + Add Elements icon, go to the Advanced section, and drag the Background Video element into your hero section.

steps.heading: Upload your video file
steps.body: Click the element to open its settings, then click Upload and select a compressed file under the 30MB size limit.

steps.heading: Set the display height
steps.body: In the right sidebar style panel, assign the video box a height, such as 60vh or 100%.

steps.heading: Layer content over the video
steps.body: Drag a Div Block inside the video element to hold your heading text and buttons, setting it to absolute position with a semi-transparent background for contrast.

steps.heading: Add a mobile fallback image
steps.body: Return to the video settings to upload a fallback image for lower-bandwidth mobile devices, then preview across breakpoints to check the loop behavior.

faq.question: What's the maximum file size for a background video?
faq.answer: Webflow's native background video upload is capped at 30MB, so your source file needs to be compressed before uploading.

faq.question: Why does my text look hard to read over the video?
faq.answer: Add a semi-transparent overlay Div Block behind your text with a dark or tinted background to boost contrast.

faq.question: Do mobile devices actually play the background video?
faq.answer: You can set a fallback image specifically for lower-bandwidth mobile devices instead of the full video to keep load times reasonable.

faq.question: Can I add buttons on top of the video, not just text?
faq.answer: Yes, any element like buttons or links can be nested inside the same overlay Div Block placed over the video.
